meta.description: Re-derived per-task/per-model/per-effort/per-run dataset from lattice-*/scores.json, reading hit counts directly rather than trusting matrix/task-breakdown.json (which silently drops single-run cells: fable@medium, fable@xhigh, haiku@none).

four_runs_investigation.verdict: NO cell in the final dataset has 4 genuine independent repeats. Every cell is either 3 repeats (12 cells) or 1 repeat (3 cells: fable@medium, fable@xhigh, haiku@none). The likely source of the "4 runs" recollection: 6 of the 12 three-repeat cells (opus@medium, opus@high, opus@xhigh, sonnet@high, sonnet@xhigh, sonnet@medium) are not native single-session repeats=3 runs -- they were assembled post-hoc from separate multi-machine handoffs, per explicit consolidation_provenance metadata in each cell's run-meta.json.
four_runs_investigation.mechanism: 5 cells (opus@medium, opus@high, opus@xhigh, sonnet@high, sonnet@xhigh) were spliced from a "1x" source (one earlier single-repeat run) + a "2x" source (a later 2-repeat run from a second machine): 1x -> r1, 2x's r1/r2 -> r2/r3. sonnet@medium was a straight copy of an already-complete native "3x" run handed off from a second machine.

evidence: lattice-opus-medium-1x / lattice-opus-medium-batch1-adjudicate: same run (seed 7742485137540372977, started 2026-07-10T23:30:19Z), covering 8/17 tasks
-- these three batches sum to 17/17 tasks and are ONE logical repeat (chunked by task-subset across 3 CLI invocations, not 3 independent repeats); this became final r1
lattice-opus-medium-2x: a separate, later run (created 2026-07-12T22:03:35Z per consolidation_provenance) supplying 2 more repeats, spliced in as r2 and r3
clearest_case_opus_medium.directory_footprint_at_peak: Around 2026-07-12 22:00-23:00Z, up to 5 separate "opus-medium"-named run directories coexisted in results/ at once (lattice-opus-medium [=1x], lattice-opus-medium-batch1-adjudicate, lattice-opus-medium-part2, lattice-opus-medium-part3, lattice-opus-medium-2x) before consolidation. This is almost certainly what produced the "4 runs" impression: skimming `ls results/` mid-consolidation would show 4-5 opus-medium-named directories, not 4 statistical repeats of one cell.
clearest_case_opus_medium.integrity_check: Verified final lattice-opus-medium/scores.json repeats_detail.per_repeat.r1.tasks['aicg-t2'] == source lattice-opus-medium-batch1-adjudicate/scores.json tasks['aicg-t2'] exactly (cold_hits=3, loaded_hits=4, n_must_hits=4), and r2 != r3 (no duplication artifact from the splice). The final repeats=3 is a correct 1x+2x=3 splice, not an undercount of a true 4.

four_runs_investigation.search_performed: grepped all of results/ (including _audit, _superseded, unavailable, concordance, gate-*, smoke-gate-*) for "repeats": 4, and for any "r4" key in any scores.json/consumer/judge-outputs filename; none found anywhere. Checked git history of results/ (gitignored except one initial-commit snapshot from 2026-07-12T02:53, which predates the 2x handoffs and shows opus-medium mid-history as the 1x/batch1 8-task single run). No genuine 4-repeat cell exists at any point in the recoverable history.

meta.known_bug_confirmed: matrix/task-breakdown.json omits fable@medium, fable@xhigh, haiku@none (and the aborted/empty fable@max) entirely from its per-task "tasks" data -- they appear only in the "cells" list, not populated in any task entry. Confirmed by inspection.

fable@medium.derivation: tasks (single run; no hit_rates/repeats_detail field exists in this scores.json for a 1-repeat cell -- top-level "tasks" IS the raw single-run record, verified equivalent)

fable@xhigh.derivation: tasks (single run; no hit_rates/repeats_detail field exists in this scores.json for a 1-repeat cell -- top-level "tasks" IS the raw single-run record, verified equivalent)

haiku@none.derivation: tasks (single run; no hit_rates/repeats_detail field exists in this scores.json for a 1-repeat cell -- top-level "tasks" IS the raw single-run record, verified equivalent)
